content.text:
<user_query>
Act as a UI Officer / Head of Design Systems for BIQc. Review component/layout/navigation and current styling direction (Claude-like objective). Identify visual consistency gaps, component debt, state handling inconsistency, mobile/desktop parity issues, and design-token governance needs. Return: (1) top 10 UI findings, (2) design system backlog, (3) visual QA protocol for terminal evidence, (4) 2026+ UI platform direction.
</user_query>
Exploring the frontend structure, styling approach, navigation, and design tokens to produce a design-systems audit.

Here is a concise design-system audit based on the current BIQc frontend in this worktree (`C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend`), aligned with a **Claude-like** target: calm neutrals, restrained chrome, one clear accent, typographic discipline, and predictable density.

---

## (1) Top 10 UI findings

1. **Three parallel color systems**  
   BIQc tokens live in `:root` / `[data-theme="light"]` in `frontend/src/index.css` (`--biqc-*` plus semantic aliases like `--bg-primary`). Separately, shadcn uses HSL variables (`--background`, `--primary`, …) under `@layer base` in the same file. They are **not clearly driven by the same theme switch**, so light/dark parity for Radix/shadcn surfaces is fragile compared to BIQc-tagged inline styles.

2. **Global `!important` overrides fight the design system**  
   Rules such as `.bg-white { background-color: hsl(222, 47%, 10%) !important; }` (see `frontend/src/index.css` around the “Force dark backgrounds on Shadcn components” section) force a dark look for **any** `bg-white` usage. That is a blunt instrument: it conflicts with `[data-theme="light"]`, marketing pages, and any legitimate white surface, and it is the opposite of token-governed styling.

3. **Token governance doc vs. implementation**  
   `frontend/src/design-system/tokens.js` states policies like unified Inter typography and avoiding arbitrary inline sizing; in practice, large surfaces still use **inline `style={{}}`**, Tailwind arbitrary colors (`text-[#F4F7FA]`), and raw hex. Example: `frontend/src/pages/LoginSupabase.js` and `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.js` mix `var(--biqc-*)`, hex, and Tailwind color classes.

4. **Typography stack is split three ways**  
   `tokens.js` exports Inter for display/body/mono. `frontend/tailwind.config.js` still maps `display` / `heading` to **Cormorant Garamond** and `mono` to **JetBrains Mono**. `LoadingScreen` in `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.js` uses Cormorant for copy and JetBrains for the “B” mark. That undercuts a single “product voice” and a Claude-like **one-family** feel.

5. **Semantic CSS variable families used inconsistently**  
   `index.css` defines `--bg-*` / `--text-*` as aliases of `--biqc-*`. Some screens use `--biqc-*` only (`SoundboardPanel.js`, parts of `DashboardLayout.js`); `MySoundBoard.js` leans on `--bg-primary`, `--text-muted`, etc. Both are valid but **mixed in sibling features**, which increases drift when tokens change.

6. **Hardcoded hex escapes the token layer**  
   Examples: `#FF6A00` brand blocks in `DashboardLayout.js` (header logo), `#0D1420` in `SoundboardPanel.js`, `#050505` full-screen states in `ProtectedRoute.js`, and the error boundary in `frontend/src/App.js`. These **do not respond** to theme or future token tweaks without another sweep.

7. **Navigation configuration is duplicated and divergent**  
   `frontend/src/components/MobileNav.js` defines `NAV_ITEMS`, a static `MORE_SECTIONS`, and a **runtime `moreSections`** with different labels (e.g. “Market & Positioning” vs “Market & Position”) and tier gating. `DashboardLayout.js` builds a separate `navSections` tree. This is **high risk for mobile/desktop parity** and copy drift.

8. **shadcn primitives vs. bespoke UI**  
   `frontend/src/components/ui/button.jsx` uses standard CVA + `bg-primary` (HSL tokens). Many product screens use **custom buttons** with inline styles instead of wrapping `Button`. That splits **focus, hover, disabled, and density** behavior.

9. **Loading / error / empty states are not theme-aligned**  
   `LoadingScreen` uses `var(--biqc-bg)` but also hardcoded `#64748B` for secondary text (below the AA-oriented `--biqc-text-muted` rationale in tokens). `AuthError` / `AccessDenied` use `bg-[#050505]` and do not clearly participate in `data-theme`.

10. **Mobile layout globals are aggressive**  
    `index.css` includes broad mobile rules (e.g. forcing `.grid-cols-3` / `.md:grid-cols-3` to a single column under `max-width: 639px`). That may fix some pages but is **global behavior** that can surprise layouts that need multi-column grids on small screens.

---

## (2) Design system backlog (prioritized)

| Priority | Item |
|----------|------|
| P0 | **Single theme API**: Either drive shadcn HSL vars from the same source as `--biqc-*` when `data-theme` changes, or stop using HSL `--primary`/`--background` for app chrome and standardize on one approach. |
| P0 | **Remove or scope `!important` hacks** on `.bg-white` / `.rounded-xl.bg-white`; replace with component-level tokens or a `data-surface` pattern. |
| P0 | **Navigation SSOT**: One exported nav config (sections, paths, labels, tier rules) consumed by `DashboardLayout` and `MobileNav`. |
| P1 | **Primitive components**: `AppButton`, `AppCard`, `AppInput` that map to tokens + shadcn behavior; migrate high-traffic flows (login, shell, soundboard). |
| P1 | **Typography decision**: Product = Inter-only per `tokens.js`; reserve serif for marketing routes only—update Tailwind `fontFamily` and purge stray Cormorant/JetBrains from app shell and auth. |
| P1 | **Ban-list lint**: ESLint or CI rule flagging raw hex / `rgb()` in `src/pages` and `src/components` (allowlist for OAuth brand SVGs). |
| P2 | **State pattern library**: Loading, error, empty, offline—same layout, same motion, same token usage; replace one-off screens. |
| P2 | **Radius & density scale**: Map `rounded-*` usage to `tokens.js` `radius` / `radiusClass` and document one default for cards vs chips. |
| P2 | **Legacy CSS cleanup**: `frontend/src/App.css` still contains **forest/lime** sidebar and light “feature-card” patterns; isolate to marketing or delete if unused. |
| P3 | **Documentation**: Short “Token map” (which var for surface vs border vs muted text) + “When to use shadcn vs BIQc inline” decision tree. |

---

## (3) Visual QA protocol (terminal / automation evidence)

Use this so evidence is **reproducible and comparable** (your repo already references Playwright screenshots and `.screenshots` naming patterns in `test_reports/`).

1. **Viewport matrix**  
   - Desktop: 1440×900 and 1920×1080.  
   - Mobile: 390×844 (iOS-ish) and 360×800.  
   - Tablet: 768×1024 optional.

2. **Theme matrix**  
   For each critical route: capture **dark and light** (`data-theme` / theme toggle in `DashboardLayout.js`), because shadcn and BIQc paths may diverge.

3. **Route checklist (shell + hero flows)**  
   e.g. post-login home, soundboard, advisor, calibration, forensic calibration, market, login—aligned with files you already snapshot (`live-visual-*`, `local-visual-*`, `qa-live-*` in git status).

4. **DOM assertions before screenshot**  
   Require `data-testid` on shell pieces (you already have many, e.g. `mobile-menu-toggle`, `mobile-more-sheet`, `auth-loading-screen`). Assert presence + no `document.body` horizontal overflow.

5. **Artifact contract**  
   - Folder: e.g. `.screenshots/<run-id>/`.  
   - Names: `<env>-<viewport>-<theme>-<route>.png`.  
   - Pair with **JSON**: viewport, theme, URL, timestamp, git SHA.  
   - **Known caveat** (from your own `test_reports/iteration_71.json`): Playwright screenshot width can misrepresent mobile bottom nav—**supplement** with `page.evaluate` checks for `MobileNav` visibility or fixed bar height, not only pixels.

6. **Regression**  
   Optional: pixel diff against baseline; block on large unexpected diffs for P0 routes.

7. **Accessibility smoke**  
   Automated pass for contrast on token text (especially anything still using `#64748B` on dark).

---

## (4) 2026+ UI platform direction (Claude-like + BIQc)

- **One neutral canvas, one accent**: Keep orange as the single high-saturation signal; push everything else toward **charcoal/slate surfaces** already defined in `tokens.js` / `index.css`. Reduce competing gradients and one-off hex “energy.”

- **Typography as infrastructure**: Commit to **Inter everywhere** in the authenticated product; use weight and size—not serif—for hierarchy. Reserve display serif for marketing if brand requires it.

- **Token runtime as law**: Components should read **CSS variables only** at render for colors; JS `colors` in `tokens.js` for charts or SVGs only. Eliminate parallel HSL vs hex mental models for the same surface.

- **Composable shell**: Sticky header + optional side rail + main + optional assistant column is close to “AI product” norms (Claude, Linear, etc.). Your `DashboardLayout` resizable sidebar/soundboard is directionally right; **standardize spacing and z-index** so overlays (notifications, drawers) feel like one system.

- **Light mode as a first-class product**: If you ship light theme, **shadcn variables must track** `data-theme`; otherwise retire light mode until they do.

- **AI-native surfaces**: Conversation threads, citations, and tool status should share **one message/list primitive** and motion vocabulary—reduces “chat glued onto BI” feeling.

---

**Files worth treating as canonical vs. debt:**  
- Canonical: `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\index.css` (BIQc tokens + theme), `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\design-system\tokens.js`.  
- High-debt hotspots: `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\ProtectedRoute.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\MobileNav.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\DashboardLayout.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\pages\LoginSupabase.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\pages\MySoundBoard.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\SoundboardPanel.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\tailwind.config.js`.

I am in **Ask mode** (read-only); if you want this turned into tracked tickets or automated checks in the repo, switch to Agent mode and we can implement governance step by step.
